Description:
The Ammaroo Phosphate Project is a two-stage development: Stage 1 proposes a phosphate rock and extraction open cut mine at the Ammaroo deposit and a beneficiation and chemical plant complex built on the Ammaroo mining leases. Stage 2 aims to see the development of a value-added processing facility designed to produce granulated fertilisers for use within Australia and for export to targeted markets in Asia.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $3.5 billion and would create about 250 construction jobs and 375 ongoing jobs.
Verdant Minerals Pty Ltd

The Australia-Asia PowerLink Project proposes a renewable energy project located in the Barkly region of the Northern Territory, approximately 800km south of Darwin. Up to 6GW of clean electricity is intended to be delivered to support decarbonisation and green industrial development in Darwin and Southeast Asia.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $60 billion and would create about 1,750 construction jobs and 350 ongoing jobs.
SunCable Holdings Pty Ltd

Project proponent: Australian Renewable Energy Hub Pty Ltd
Description:
AREH proposes the development of renewable power and green hydrogen to support decarbonisation of the Pilbara and beyond. The Project is planned to be developed in phases, initially focused on supplying renewable power and green hydrogen to customers in the Pilbara. Future phases will look to produce additional domestic green hydrogen and also supply green ammonia for export customers.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $30 billion and would create about 3,000 ongoing jobs.
AREH

Project name: Bonaparte Carbon Capture and Storage Project
Project proponent: INPEX Browse E&P Australia Pty Ltd
Description:
The Bonaparte Carbon Capture and Storage Project is intended to be located at the Darwin Middle Arm Sustainable Development Precinct (MASDP) and connected via a 250 km undersea pipeline to an injection site west of Darwin.
The Project is expected to sequester a minimum of 10 million tonnes of CO2 per annum over its 20-year lifespan.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $3.5 billion and would create about 370 construction jobs and 40 ongoing jobs.
INPEX

The Project is in the Gippsland offshore wind zone, approximately 10km from Seaspray, Victoria. Blue Mackerel propose to construct and operate up to 69 wind turbines with an installed capacity of 1GW - providing a sustainable and low emissions solution to support Australia’s transition to a cleaner future for up to 40 years.
The Project has an estimated capital expenditure of $6.5 billion and would create about 1500 construction jobs and 200 ongoing jobs.
Parkwind Jera

The Broken Hill Cobalt Project aims to produce high purity cobalt sulphate from a large cobalt deposit near Broken Hill, NSW, for use in the manufacture of lithium ion batteries globally. The Project also aims to produce elemental sulphur, a key ingredient in fertiliser production, to help reduce Australia’s dependence on fertiliser imports.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.728 billion and would create about 20 construction jobs and 350 ongoing jobs.
Cobalt Blue Holdings Limited

The Cape Hardy Green Hydrogen Project is a proposed green hydrogen and ammonia production facility located at Cape Hardy on the eastern coast of the Eyre Peninsula in South Australia.
The facility intends to generate renewable energy through a mix of wind and solar over two stages. Stage 1 aims for 1 GW electrolyser capacity, with an incremental increase of 4GW in electrolyser capacity in stage 2, giving a total of 5GW electrolyser capacity.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $35 billion and would create about 2,000 construction jobs and 500 ongoing jobs.
Revera Energy

Project name: Donald Rare Earth and Minerals Project
Description:
The Donald Rare Earth and Mineral Sands Project, located in Victoria’s Murray Basin near Minyip and Donald, is set to become a globally significant source of critical minerals. With a resource exceeding 2.4 billion tonnes of ore containing zirconium, titanium, and rare earth elements such as neodymium and praseodymium, the Project will support technologies vital to clean energy, defence, and advanced manufacturing.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.392 billion and would create about 100 construction jobs and 220 ongoing jobs.
Astron Limited

The Fingerboards Mineral Sands Project is a critical minerals development expected to produce approximately 200 tpa of heavy rare earth oxides, including dysprosium and terbium. It will also deliver around 1,800 tpa of light rare earth oxides such as neodymium and praseodymium, along with about 75,000 tpa of zircon.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.295 billion and would create about 400 construction jobs and 262 ongoing jobs.
Gippsland Critical Minerals

The Gonneville Critical Minerals Project proposes a critical minerals mining and processing development located on Proponent-owned farmland 70Kms northeast of Perth.
The Project is targeting platinum group elements (PGE) minerals that are critical for decarbonisation and the clean energy transformation, with growing demand for use in electric vehicles, energy storage, renewable energy technologies and green hydrogen.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $1.6 billion and would create about 1,200 construction jobs and 500 ongoing jobs.
Chalice Mining Limited

Project name: Goschen Rare Earth Mineral Sands Project
Description:
The Goschen Rare Earths Mineral Sands Project proposes an integrated critical minerals mining and processing asset in Victoria’s Loddon Mallee Region. The Project aims to develop a combination of rare earth oxide products, mixed heavy rare earth carbonate concentrates, and zirconium and titanium minerals.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.606 billion and would create about 200 construction jobs and 400 ongoing jobs.
VHM Limited

The Goongarrie Nickel-Cobalt Hub is a major mining and processing initiative located approximately 75 kilometres north of Kalgoorlie-Boulder in Western Australia’s Eastern Goldfields region.
The project proposes the development of long-life open pit mines and associated processing infrastructure to produce high-quality nickel and cobalt in the form of Mixed Sulphide Precipitate (MSP).
The project has a capital expenditure of $3.1 billion and is expected to create around 3,000 construction jobs and 800 ongoing operational jobs.
Regulatory services being provided by the Investor Front Door.
Ardea Resources Limited

Niobium is a critical mineral used primarily to strengthen steel for automotive, construction and energy infrastructure, and is increasingly important in advanced technologies such as superalloys, aerospace and defence components and medical equipment. Luni’s scale and grade position it to be able to produce niobium products for customers in established and emerging markets.
The project is finalising key project metrics, however, exceeds the Major Project Status eligibility threshold of $50 million CAPEX and is anticipated to create hundreds of jobs.
WA1 Resources Ltd

Project proponent: Murchison Hydrogen Renewables Pty Ltd as trustee for the Murchison Hydrogen Renewables Project Trust
Description:
The Murchison Project proposes a green-field, power-to-ammonia project located on the Murchison House pastoral station in the mid-west region of Western Australia. The Project would use combined onshore wind and solar energy to produce green ammonia.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $15 billion and would create about 2,500 construction and 600 ongoing jobs.
Regulatory services being provided by the Investor Front Door.
Murchison Renewables

The NiWest Project aims to produce high purity nickel and cobalt sulphate products from large nickel-cobalt deposits located in the West Australian Nickel belt. Once operational, the project is expected to produce 90,000 tonnes of nickel sulphate and 7,000 tonnes of cobalt sulphate annually for use in the manufacture of electric vehicle batteries.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $1.26 billion and would create about 600 construction jobs and 438 ongoing jobs.
Alliance Nickel Limited

The Northern Silica Project aims to produce 3 to 5 million tonnes of silica sand annually over 25 years. The high-purity silica sand mining development will supply essential materials for domestic manufacture of solar panels, silicon wafers and high-end electronics.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.535 billion and would create about 120 construction jobs and 90 ongoing jobs.
Diatreme Resources Limited

The Northern Quartz Campus project includes a 30,000 t pa capacity biocarbon production facility, a 780 MW battery energy storage system, 515 MW of solar generation and 50,000 tpa metallurgical grade silicon production facility.
The Project has an estimated capital expenditure of up to $4.5 billion and is expected to create about 1,200 construction jobs and 2,000 ongoing jobs.
Solquartz Pty Ltd

Project Ares is a proposed hyperscale data centre development at Murranji Station in the Northern Territory, approximately 370 km south of Katherine. The project will be delivered in stages, with an initial 500 MW facility powered by integrated renewable energy, with potential expansion to 1 GW.
The project has a projected capital expenditure of around A$11.9 billion and is expected to create approximately 4,400 construction jobs and up to 300 ongoing jobs.
Energy North Pty Ltd

Project wafers will establish a commercial scale 2 GW silicon ingot and sustainable photovoltaic (PV) wafer manufacturing facility to support Australia’s emerging domestic solar supply chain.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.420 billion and would create about 410 construction jobs and 300 ongoing jobs.
Stellar PV

Project Sol is a multi-stage development in the Northern Territory, designed to produce large-scale green ammonia through a phased rollout - starting at 500 Ktpa and scaling to 4.7 Mtpa across four stages.
The project will utilise behind-the-metre solar and Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in the Barkly region, supported by underground hydrogen pipeline infrastructure along the proposed Territory Energy Link corridor, transporting hydrogen to processing facilities at the Middle Arm Precinct near Darwin.
Green ammonia output is targeted for export to Japan, Korea, Singapore, and Europe. With a projected capital expenditure of $35 billion, Project Sol is expected to create around 3,000 construction jobs and 500 ongoing jobs.
Energy North

The Razorback Iron Ore Project will produce 5 million tonnes per annum (Mtpa) of high-purity iron ore concentrate with a current mineral resource estimate of over three billion tonnes that includes up to two billion tonnes of probable ore reserves.
The project is part of the Green Iron SA consortium which intends to make South Australia a global leader in the low-carbon steel supply chain.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $1.86 billion and would create about 1,474 construction jobs and 450 ongoing jobs.
Magnetite Mines Ltd

The Siviour Battery Anode Materials Project proposes an integrated graphite mine and battery anode material operation with a mine and concentrator located in South Australia's Eyre Peninsula, and a downstream purified spherical graphite manufacturing facility located in Port Adelaide. The Project would contribute to the global battery industry and electric vehicle markets.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.609 billion and would create about 200 construction jobs and 400 ongoing jobs.
Renascor Resources Limited

The Speewah Project, located in the East Kimberley region of Western Australia, proposes a staged approach to producing commercial grade fluorspar and vanadium electrolyte from two critical mineral deposits of fluorite and vanadium titanomagnetite.
Once operational, the Project aims to produce commercial grade fluorspar for use in electric vehicle and semiconductor manufacturing, contributing to diversification of Australia’s supply chains as the only fluorspar producer in Australia. Vanadium electrolyte is intended to be produced from vanadium titanomagnetite in a subsequent stage for use in vanadium redox flow batteries.
The first stage of the Project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.236 billion and would create about 300 construction jobs and 150 ongoing jobs.
Tivan Limited

Project name: Star of the South Offshore Wind Farm
Project proponent: Star of the South Wind Farm Pty Ltd
Description:
The Star of the South Offshore Wind Farm Project, located off the Gippsland coast of Victoria, aims to be Australia’s first offshore wind project. When fully operational, the Project is expected to supply up to 2.2 gigawatts of renewable energy to the National Electricity Market, contributing up to 20 percent of Victoria’s electricity.
The Project comprises both offshore and onshore assets, including wind turbines, substation and platforms, a network of subsea cables, underground and overhead powerlines, and modified ports.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $8.7 billion and would create about 2,280 construction jobs and 300 ongoing jobs.
Star of the South Wind Farm Pty Ltd

The Sunlands Eyre Peninsula Graphite Hub Project proposes to re-establish mining and processing of flake graphite at Uley on South Australia’s Eyre Peninsula.
When fully operational the Project is projected to produce up to 200,000 tonnes of concentrate per annum for export for battery anode manufacturing.
The project has a projected capital expenditure of $0.385 billion and would create about 100 constructions jobs and 180 ongoing jobs.
Quantum Graphite Limited

The Sunrise Battery Materials Complex, located near Parkes in New South Wales, proposes mining nickel and cobalt along with the rare alloying metal scandium, to produce high-purity sulphates for the rapidly expanding lithium-ion battery market. At full capacity the Project is expected to produce 25,000 of nickel and 7000 tonnes of cobalt per annum as high-purity sulphates for direct supply to battery manufacturers in Asia, Europe and America.
The Project has a projected capital expenditure of $2.609 billion and would create about 1,900 construction jobs and 380 ongoing jobs.
Sunrise Energy Metals Limited

The WAH2 Clean Ammonia Project is a proposed low emissions ammonia production development located in the Maitland Strategic Industrial Area near Karratha in Western Australia. The project will produce low emissions ammonia for use as a marine fuel, supporting the transition to cleaner shipping and enabling onshore bunkering at the Port of Dampier.
The project has a projected capital expenditure of approximately A$3.2 billion and is expected to create around 550 construction jobs and approximately 200 ongoing jobs.
NH3 Clean Energy Ltd

The Western Australia Rare Earths Refinery is a proposed industrial facility located in the Rockingham Industrial Zone near Perth, Western Australia. The project will process rare earth monazite concentrate into mixed rare earth carbonate, supporting Australia’s capability in rare earth processing and strengthening its role in global critical minerals supply chains.
The project has a projected capital expenditure of approximately A$511.3 million and is expected to create around 1,100 construction jobs and approximately 100 ongoing jobs.
Tronox Management Pty Ltd

The Western Green Energy Hub (WGEH) is a proposed large-scale renewable energy project located on the Nullarbor Plain in the far southeast of Western Australia.
At full scale, the WGEH is projected to produce 70 gigawatts of electricity from wind and solar, with capacity to produce up to 3.5 million tonnes of green hydrogen and derivatives annually.
The project has a projected capital expenditure of $70 billion and would create about 3000 construction jobs and 500 ongoing jobs.
WGEH Pty Ltd

The Wingellina Nickel-Cobalt Project will span Western Australia, South Australia, and the Northern Territory. The Project proposes an integrated mining and processing facility in Western Australia that will produce Mixed Hydroxide Precipitate (MHP) from high-grade nickel-cobalt limonite.
MHP is a key raw material used to produce lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles and energy storage units. MHP will be transported to port for domestic use and for export to global customers.
Once operational, the project will produce nickel and cobalt for a minimum of 42 years.
The project has a projected capital expenditure of $2.9 billion and would create about 1,500 construction jobs and 500 ongoing jobs.
NiCo Resources Limited
